name: neural-weights-en-nz
$comment_662: 6.6.2 (2026-07-24) introduces the en-nz overlay on the SAME unchanged v385 encoder as 6.6.1: no new model bytes anywhere, one new decode-time artifact (pair-index-nz.bin, δ=10) consumed by @mailwoman/neural's probe chain. Full recovery on the NZ repeated-name class (bar 1's 100%) additionally requires @mailwoman/neural ≥7.8.0's identical-adjacent-segment (x,x) rule — on the pre-fix segment path the as-written board measures 74.4% tag-correct (the 63 same-name suburb/town rows fuse); the comma-free anchored path is unaffected either way.
$comment_new_card: Overlay-LOCAL card (same posture as en-gb's since the 7.6.0 cycle). `labels`/`requires`/`architecture`/`components_supported` are duplicated VERBATIM from the base card so decode behavior matches the base exactly — keep in lockstep on every base ship.
model_lineage: Data-only overlay. Ships NO model.onnx/tokenizer.model of its own — resolves both from `@mailwoman/neural-weights-en-us` via `mailwoman.baseWeights` (byte-identical artifact; one encoder serves both locales). What this package ships: `pair-index-nz.bin` (PIX1 placetype-pair retrieval index, built from the LINZ-derived OpenAddresses NZ countrywide CSV, δ=10) plus the shared gazetteer/country lexicons. UNLIKE en-gb there is NO postcode-nz.bin — see no_postcode_bin below.
phase: en-nz battery PASS (2026-07-24, all 6 pre-registered bars — task-8 report § 'en-nz battery'). NZ dependent_locality goes live via the decode-time placetype-pair prior on the untouched shipped v385 base, same Option-A recipe as GB: v385's dead-classifier deficit on the NZ board is large but uniform (raw-BIO gap mean 7.817, min 5.790, any-fire 0/246 — same shape as GB's 7.024), so δ=10 clears the board at saturation (identical plateau to δ=12/15). The repeated-name suburb/town class (NZ-specific, 63/246 rows) is recovered by @mailwoman/neural 7.8.0's identical-adjacent-segment rule.

base_model.$comment: v385 bytes (model.onnx md5 700f3cf4c08f31536f0bc053c281b19e) — this overlay adds decode-time data only, the encoder does not move.
base_model.resolution: mailwoman.baseWeights in package.json — resolveWeights/resolveFromPackageDir resolve model.onnx + tokenizer.model from the base package dir (source suffixed "+base"); this card's own labels/requires/architecture below are a verbatim copy of the base's, not re-derived at runtime — keep them in lockstep on every base ship (same discipline as en-gb's card).

pair_index_nz_bin.format: PIX1 placetype-pair index — a retrieval-augmented (child, parent) dependent_locality prior, e.g. ("Plimmerton", "Porirua")
pair_index_nz_bin.source: LINZ-derived OpenAddresses NZ countrywide CSV (the census the synth-nz-v2 shard was built from), $MAILWOMAN_DATA_ROOT/openaddresses/extracted/nz/countrywide.csv, source md5 fa79e586de96fe54c17b6763a9ea97c6, 2,123,054 rows kept / 137 skipped (empty CITY)
pair_index_nz_bin.delta_calibration: δ=10 (NZ δ-sweep on the SHIPPED v385, task-8 report 2026-07-24 § 'NZ arc'). Saturates at δ=10 — identical emit/correct to δ=12/15, the same flat-plateau shape as GB's own sweep; golden-FP 0/54 at every δ tested. The sweep table lives in eval.delta_sweep_v385 below.
pair_index_nz_bin.repeated_name_convention: NZ's register genuinely repeats names across the suburb/town tiers: 255/1178 names (21.6%) form identity pairs (child == parent, e.g. Mangawhai / Mangawhai) — the target population of @mailwoman/neural 7.8.0's identical-adjacent-segment (x,x) rule, which splits two identical adjacent segments into dependent_locality + locality instead of fusing them into one span. GB has 0/19,431 identity pairs, so the rule is structurally unreachable there (verified, not assumed — see eval.gb_reproduction).
pair_index_nz_bin.country_gate: hard-gated — the prior only fires when the resolved locale's country matches the index header's country (nz). Never crosses into en-us/en-gb parses (see neural/test/weights.test.ts).
pair_index_nz_bin.not_committed: derived artifact, .gitignore'd (same posture as en-gb's pair-index-gb.bin) — rebuilt via `mailwoman gazetteer pair-index --out neural-weights-en-nz --country nz --source <linz-countrywide-csv> --delta 10`

nz_artifacts.no_postcode_bin: FOLLOW-UP: this overlay ships NO postcode-nz.bin — no WOF NZ postcode shard exists on disk or in release.config.json's softFeed.postcodeDBByCountry (unlike us/fr/gb). Until one is built, the postcode-anchor channel resolves OFF for en-nz loads (loud single warning via warnUnfedChannel, parse still works — the tolerant-loader contract). Building the NZ shard + wiring postcodeDBByCountry.nz is the tracked follow-up for a later bundle revision.

architecture.$comment: Verbatim copy of the base card's architecture — same encoder, no NZ-specific change.

requires.$comment: Verbatim copy of the base card's requires block — same trained ship-config, same fail-closed contract via createScorer. Keep in lockstep with neural-weights-en-us/model-card.json on every base ship. NOTE: anchor.required is true (the encoder is anchor-trained) but this overlay ships no postcode-nz.bin — see nz_artifacts.no_postcode_bin for the resulting anchor-OFF posture on NZ loads.

notes: 6.6.2 is the NZ replication of GB's 6.6.1 story: the prior alone on the untouched v385 base, plus one NZ-specific decode rule. The NZ suburb board's as-written correct-rate was 74.4% pre-fix — every miss traced 1-for-1 to the 63/246 rows where suburb and town share the identical name (segment mode fused both into one dependent_locality span). The (x,x) rule closes that class completely (246/246). The comma-free anchored path was never affected (99.2% both before and after — the highest tag-correct measured on any NZ or GB board in the task-8 report). Venue-confound evidence is INTERIM (see eval.synthetic_venue_fp): no real NZ venue-name source exists on disk (poi.db has zero NZ rows; no NZ Overture/OSM extract; geonames/NZ.txt is a bare postcode reference) — acquisition tracked as issue #1279. All numbers: .superpowers/sdd/task-8-report.md §§ 'NZ arc: census + δ-sweep scoping (#1277)', 'en-nz battery'.

eval.$comment: All rows measured on the SHIPPED v385 base + this package's δ=10 pair-index-nz.bin via @mailwoman/neural's auto probe chain + the 7.8.0 identical-adjacent-segment fix (2026-07-24 en-nz battery, pre-registered bars — task-8 report).

nz_suburb_board_as_written.note: pre-fix baseline 246 emit / 183 correct (74.4%) — the 63 repeated-name rows were the recovery target and are fully recovered.

nz_suburb_board_comma_stripped.note: the anchored-adjacent path; byte-identical pre-fix and post-fix (the fix correctly leaves it alone).

synthetic_venue_fp.board: nz-venue-interim-board.jsonl, 510 rows — 170 census pairs × 3 civic/business-name templates
synthetic_venue_fp.status: INTERIM — synthetic templates, NOT real venue-name data. No FSA-equivalent NZ business-name source exists on disk (verified by direct search); read as 'the interim board found no problem', not 'there is no problem'. Real-data acquisition tracked as issue #1279; a real-source re-measure is required before any non-interim FP claim ships.

delta_sweep_v385.$comment: NZ δ-sweep, auto chain, as-written 246-row suburb board (emit/tag-correct, PRE-fix segment path) + golden-FP of 54 no-suburb rows.

eval.gb_reproduction: every GB number reproduces to the exact integer through the 7.8.0 (x,x) fix, both probe-chain legs: gb-golden 69/69 emit / 69/69 correct, comma-stripped 50/69 emit / 47/50 (94.0%), golden no-DL FP 0/51, venue FP 217/6500 (segment leg) + 4/6500 (anchored leg). Corroborated structurally: GB's census has 0/19,431 identity pairs, so the rule cannot fire on GB at all.
eval.presets_and_golden: 6 demo presets + golden us (n=2660) / fr (n=1546) byte-identical through the fix; invariance suite 138 INVARIANT / 6 DEGRADED / 12 LOST — 0 NEW violation classes vs the v385 baseline (identical profile to the pre-fix battery).

format.$comment: Model/tokenizer format is the base's — see neural-weights-en-us/model-card.json. This overlay's own artifact:
